Design system  /  Print  /  One-pager

Leave-behind one-pager

A4 double-sided. White front, cobalt-900 back, both English. Structured like a Unix manpage: NAME, SYNOPSIS, DESCRIPTION, EXAMPLES, BUGS. Confident, MKB-direct, anti-marketing. The leave-behind that gets pinned to the wall.

$ man conduction(7) EN · page 1/2
Conduction

Name

conduction — OSS product company. Builds apps for Nextcloud that keep you in control of your own data.

Synopsis

conduction install [OpenCatalogi | OpenRegister | OpenConnector | DocuDesk | MyDash] [--solution=woo|procurement|public]

Description

We build five apps. Not "a platform". Not "a solution". Apps you install from the Nextcloud app store, on a server you run. Open-source, MIT/AGPL, no vendor lock-in.

On top of the apps sit solutions, concrete outcomes built on those apps. A WOO publication setup. A procurement workflow. A public catalogue. Apps are the building blocks, solutions are what you explain to your board.

Examples

  • WOO publication: OpenCatalogi + OpenConnector + DocuDesk, live in 8 weeks.
  • Open registers: OpenRegister loaded with your own schema, audit trail out of the box.
  • Personal start page for staff: MyDash next to the Nextcloud tiles.

Who

PrimarySMB decision-maker, IT lead, 10–500 people, finds us via Google. Wants to install today.
SecondaryPublic-sector decision-maker, municipalities and joint arrangements. Procurement in months, compliance signals required.
TertiaryDeveloper / integrator. Wants the API spec and the licence one click away.

Bugs

We have them. File at codeberg.org/Conduction and we'll fix them. We don't send sales after an issue, only a commit.

See also

conduction.nl · codeberg.org/Conduction · docs.conduction.nl

Conduction BV · KvK 50615773 · Zoetermeer v2026.04 · EN
Front · A4 · English · manpage(7) style
$ man conduction(7) EN · page 2/2
Conduction

Name

conduction — Dutch OSS product company. Builds apps for Nextcloud that keep you in control of your own data.

Synopsis

conduction install [OpenCatalogi | OpenRegister | OpenConnector | DocuDesk | MyDash] [--solution=woo|procurement|public]

Description

We build five apps. Not "a platform". Not "a solution". Apps you install from the Nextcloud app store, on a server you run. Open-source, MIT/AGPL, zero vendor lock-in.

Solutions sit on top: a WOO transparency setup, a procurement workflow, a public catalogue. Apps are the building blocks, solutions are what you explain to a council.

Examples

  • WOO publication: OpenCatalogi + OpenConnector + DocuDesk, eight weeks to live.
  • Open registers: OpenRegister loaded with your own schema, audit trail out of the box.
  • Personal start page for staff: MyDash next to the Nextcloud tiles.

Who

PrimarySMB decision-maker, IT lead, 10–500 people, finds us via Google. Wants to install today.
SecondaryPublic-sector decision-maker, municipalities and joint arrangements. Procurement in months, compliance signals required.
TertiaryDeveloper / integrator. Wants the API spec and the licence one click away.

Bugs

We have them. File at codeberg.org/Conduction and we will fix them. We do not send sales after an issue, only a commit.

See also

conduction.nl · codeberg.org/Conduction · docs.conduction.nl

Conduction BV · CoC 50615773 · Zoetermeer, NL v2026.04 · EN
Back · A4 · English · cobalt-900 ground for contrast
Why we built it this way

A leave-behind that doesn't lie

Most one-pagers read like a brochure: claims, gradients, vague verbs. Ours reads like a Unix manpage: NAME, SYNOPSIS, DESCRIPTION, EXAMPLES, BUGS. The structure is the message.

  • Manpage structure. Tech buyers grok it instantly. Non-tech buyers find it refreshingly direct, "ah, the Bugs section is honest". Either way, memorable.
  • Apps vs solutions, on the page. The DESCRIPTION section pins down our taxonomy: apps are downloadable, solutions are outcomes built on apps. Clearest possible answer to "what do you actually sell".
  • BUGS section, plural. An anti-marketing move. Builds trust faster than three case-studies.
  • "What's next" not "Contact us". "File issues at codeberg.org/Conduction and we'll fix them", we send a commit, not a sales rep.
  • Inverted contrast on the back. Front on white, back on cobalt-900. The back becomes the night-mode side, signals "flip me" without arrows or "see other side". And it forces a clear front/back distinction at a glance.
  • One orange accent, on the dark side only. The hex on the back cover is orange, the front hex is cobalt. The orange does its job (one-per-screen) without breaking the rule across both sides.
  • Plex Mono for section heads. SECTION-CASE titles in mono, like a real manpage. Plus they stop looking like marketing slides.
A4 portrait · double-sided · bleed 0 · both sides English; the back reuses the same component layout with the ground colour flipped to cobalt-900
Uses
tokens.css · hex-bullet icon · .paper--A4 double-sided · Plex Mono for section heads · MKB-direct copy from voice
Download .odtLibreOffice / Word →

Open in your editor, fill the {{ placeholders }}, export to PDF.
